generated_comment: Arre bhai, company ka bhi ek hisaab hota hai. Aaj kal young girls ko hire karo toh management pehle se sochta hai maternity leave, period leave, phir childcare flexibility — project continuity kaun sambhalega? Equality chahiye toh policy bhi balanced honi chahiye, aise sirf men pe extra load daalna fair nahi hai.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it frames women as workplace liabilities due to menstruation and maternity, while presenting the bias as a fairness and productivity concern. It encodes economic framing, reverse discrimination framing, and the assumption that young women will soon need maternity-related accommodations.
original_frame: Motherhood as Basis of Respect, Women Defined by Male Relations
persona_text: You are Mohammed. Age 48. Married. A Muslim man in Hyderabad. Your 26 year old daughter Sana has told you and your wife that she and her husband have decided to wait at least three more years before having children because they both want to establish their careers first. You are sitting with your wife after Sana has left. You are telling your wife what you think of this decision and what it means about the kind of wife Sana is and what Islam says about a woman's role in marriage and why you think Sana's husband is not guiding her correctly.
